tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D confirmed: no script auto-advances .current_phase after gate passes; remained at 0 after all phase1 steps complete. Manually patched phase-state.json to current_phase=2 to continue protocol.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after Phase 1 gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: init project; --start-phase1; complete all 5 phase1 steps; run check-phase-gate.sh; observe .current_phase still 0 in .claude/phase-state.json; grep scripts/ for any 'current_phase=' assignment to 1/2/3 yields no automated advancement helper
framework_bugs.expected: After Phase 1 steps complete and gate passes (with approval logged), some documented script should bump .current_phase from 0/1 to 2
framework_bugs.actual: .current_phase remained at 0; no script in scripts/ exposes a --advance-phase flag; users must manually edit phase-state.json

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: U-C confirmation: direct shell `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'` succeeded — there is no commit-msg hook installed in the project. PreToolUse pre-commit-gate.sh DOES correctly deny when invoked through the hook payload (verified separately by piping {tool_input.command:'git commit -m "feat(...): ..."'} into pre-commit-gate.sh: returned permissionDecision=deny).

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1 + --complete-step build_loop:feature_recorded, started new feature 'uat-feat-grace', completed all 5 steps, then PreToolUse pre-commit-gate emitted no decision (allow). PR #17 grace window reset works.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Wrote .claude/pending-approval.json; pre-commit-gate piped any commit returned permissionDecision=deny with sentinel message

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed sentinel; subsequent gate invocation returned no decision (allow)

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: .claude/build-progress.json .features_completed contains 'uat-feat-1'

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: direct shell git commit bypasses BL-006 (no commit-msg hook)
framework_bugs.reproduction: In a project initialized via init.sh, run `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'` directly without invoking through Claude Code's PreToolUse path; commit succeeds despite no Build Loop being active
framework_bugs.expected: BL-006 should also block direct shell commits via .git/hooks/commit-msg or pre-commit
framework_bugs.actual: Only the PreToolUse hook (pre-commit-gate.sh) enforces BL-006; bare shell commits are unguarded. Per runbook this is the still-open U-C from prior sweep (BL-010 work item).

Phase 3 generation step did not produce a populated scenarios file in this UAT run, so this assertion is N/A. Recording as Low documentation_gap: runbook 'uat_template_lints_clean' check needs to specify the populated path or the assertion needs softening for stub-only runs.

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh exits 1 (blocking) by default — requires PROJECT_BIBLE.md and complains about missing approval gate dates in phase-state.json. Stubbed PROJECT_BIBLE.md and PRODUCT_MANIFESTO.md and re-ran with SOIF_PHASE_GATES=warn — passes RC=0. Surfaced a separate integer-comparison bug at check-phase-gate.sh lines 315 and 321 (similar pattern to U-K).

framework_bugs.summary: check-phase-gate.sh integer-cmp error on grep -c fallback (similar to U-K)
framework_bugs.reproduction: From a fresh init project, stub PROJECT_BIBLE.md with single-line content; set current_phase=2; run `bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/check-phase-gate.sh`; observe stderr lines: '/Users/.../scripts/check-phase-gate.sh: line 315: [: 0\n0: integer expression expected' and the same on line 321
framework_bugs.expected: Numeric comparisons should not error; the `grep -c PATTERN FILE 2>/dev/null || echo 0` idiom emits both '0' (from grep on no match — note grep -c returns 0 when zero matches found in some greps but the redirection captures 0\n0)
framework_bugs.actual: Lines 315 ($placeholder_dates) and 321 ($bible_sections) compare a value that contains a newline; the fallback `|| echo 0` should only fire if grep failed, not when count is zero. Recommend wrapping with `tr -d '\n'` or `head -1`.

documentation_gaps.gap: lint-uat-scenarios.sh assertion in runbook needs to point at a populated artifact path that the framework actually generates during Phase 3, OR the runbook should mark it conditional

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: Critical bug: process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 exits 1 with '[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in production mode.' This blocks all production-deployment projects from entering Phase 4. The check at scripts/process-checklist.sh line 540 only verifies poc_mode is non-null/non-empty, but production projects have poc_mode='production' written by init.sh, so the check is never satisfied for production orgs. Workaround: jq 'del(.poc_mode)' .claude/phase-state.json then start-phase4 succeeds.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: After workaround, all 6 PHASE4_STEPS (production_build, rollback_tested, go_live_verified, monitoring_configured, handoff_written, handoff_tested) completed RC=0 with stub artifacts (rollback test result, go-live test result, monitoring runbook, HANDOFF.md with Section 8, handoff test result)

tests.name: handoff_md_required
tests.details: phase4_release:monitoring_configured warned that HANDOFF.md must document monitoring; populating Section 8 cleared the warning

tests.name: release_notes_required
tests.details: RELEASE_NOTES.md stub created; gate did not specifically reject

tests.name: phase4_completion_state
tests.details: U-D regression: after all 6 phase4 steps complete, .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase remains at 3 (no script auto-advances to 4 or 'released'). Same root cause as Phase 1→2 advancement gap.

framework_bugs.reproduction: Run init.sh --non-interactive --deployment organizational --gov-mode production ... ; observe .claude/phase-state.json contains poc_mode='production'; then run `bash scripts/process-checklist.sh --start-phase4`; exits 1 with message 'Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in production mode.'
framework_bugs.expected: Production-mode projects (the only ones eligible for Phase 4) should be PERMITTED to start Phase 4. Only POC modes (private_poc, sponsored_poc, personal) should be blocked.
framework_bugs.actual: scripts/process-checklist.sh:540 condition `if [ -n "$poc_mode" ] && [ "$poc_mode" != "null" ]` blocks ALL non-null poc_mode values, including 'production'. Should be: `if [ -n "$poc_mode" ] && [ "$poc_mode" != "null" ] && [ "$poc_mode" != "production" ]`. Without this fix, production projects cannot enter Phase 4 via the documented process — they have to manually delete poc_mode from phase-state.json or use SOIF_FORCE_STEP.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only pre-existing untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ entries (the report dir itself); HEAD SHA d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 unchanged from start of run; framework-self guard from PR #18 held.

summary: Init.sh non-interactive flow + Phase 0/1/2/3/4 happy paths run end-to-end; one Critical regression found (start-phase4 incorrectly blocks production projects because poc_mode='production' is non-null); two known-open prior-sweep bugs re-confirmed (U-C BL-006 commit-msg gap, U-D phase auto-advancement gap); one new Medium integer-cmp bug in check-phase-gate.sh similar to U-K.
